topic: China social insurance obligations for foreign employees and treaty-based exemption handling
angle: Decision-focused compliance and payroll operations guide for foreign employers using EOR or direct entities

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China (2012 Amendment)
sources.notes: Used for employment relationship and written contract obligations that connect to social insurance enrollment process controls.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.notes: Core legal basis for social insurance participation obligations, contribution mechanics, and treatment of foreigners employed in China.

sources.title: Worldwide Tax Summaries: People’s Republic of China – Other taxes
sources.published_or_updated: Page timestamp as displayed by publisher
sources.notes: Practical market summary on social security treatment of foreign employees and bilateral totalization agreement handling in practice.

consensus_points: China social insurance participation is not optional once an employment relationship exists within scope.
Employer-side payroll operations must map enrollment, contribution base, and monthly filings consistently with local authority practice.
Cross-border cases can involve treaty-based exemption pathways, but documentation and local filing treatment are decisive.
uncertainties_or_interpretation_risks: City-level administrative practice can differ in implementation detail, especially document checklists and timing expectations.
Treaty-exemption handling can vary by city and insurance branch, so employers should confirm current local bureau practice before relying on historical process maps.
English translations of legal texts may not capture every nuance; Chinese originals and local advice remain the final reference for edge cases.
